codeReview.summary: The fix resolves model-provider prefixes, run_slop_code wiring, budget split application, artifact copying, and checkpoint-by-checkpoint persistence. One blocking gap remains. Reviewer output is parsed into state but never injected into the actual implementer execution path.

issues.description: run_two_agent calls build_implementer_prompt(...) but discards its returned text, then calls run_slop_code with prompt_template=implementer_prompt. Because build_implementer_prompt has no side effects, state.last_reviewer_suggestions never reaches the implementer run. This leaves the required reviewer-to-implementer propagation incomplete.

test_reviewer_output_injected checks only that state.last_reviewer_suggestions becomes non-null. It does not assert that checkpoint_2 implementer execution receives reviewer feedback, so the propagation gap above is not detected by tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The python-worker skill still conflicts with mission validator scoping, which can push workers toward full-repo validators that are intentionally out of scope for this mission.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/python-worker/SKILL.md step 6 requires `uv run pytest -q` and `uv run ruff check .`, while mission AGENTS.md 'Validator Scope' requires `uv run pytest tests/runner/ tests/analysis/ -q` and `uv run ruff check research/ tests/runner/ tests/analysis/ .factory/`.

summary: This fix commit addresses the prior model-provider-config and runner-resilience failures, and most two-agent-runner-core failures. It does not fully address reviewer propagation into subsequent implementer execution, so the review status is fail.
